---
title: "Shiny App Launch"
subtitle: "Static launch page for local or deployed Shiny access"
author: "Group Project Starter"
date: "2026-03-31"
format:
  html:
    toc: false
---

```{r}
shiny_app_url <- trimws(Sys.getenv("SHINY_APP_URL", unset = ""))
has_shiny_target <- nzchar(shiny_app_url)
is_local_target <- has_shiny_target && grepl("127\\.0\\.0\\.1|localhost", shiny_app_url)
```

## Launch Target

Current target:

`r if (has_shiny_target) shiny_app_url else "Not configured yet."`

- Mode: `r if (!has_shiny_target) "Configuration required" else if (is_local_target) "Local development" else "External deployment"`
- Recommended website entry page: [app-guide.qmd](../app-guide.qmd)

## Open The App

`r if (has_shiny_target) sprintf("[Open the Shiny app](%s){target=\"_blank\"}", shiny_app_url) else "Set SHINY_APP_URL and re-render the site before using this page as a public launch page."`

## Local Run Command

```r
shiny::runApp("app")
```

Or from the project root:

```powershell
Rscript run_app.R 3838
```

## Deployment Note

For Netlify plus `shinyapps.io`, set `SHINY_APP_URL` to the deployed app before rendering the website so the launch button points to the public target instead of an unconfigured placeholder.
